product_summary.one_sentence_summary: Industry-specific French training for Luxembourg companies that need workplace French rather than generic classroom French.
product_summary.target_user: Non-francophone employees in Luxembourg firms across finance, IT, retail, healthcare and public-sector roles.
product_summary.core_pain: Teams need French for meetings, emails and client communication without wasting time on generic content that does not match their work context.
product_summary.claimed_outcome: Better workplace French, sector vocabulary and a progress signal that can support HR reporting.

primary_buyer_type.reason: The offer is framed as a B2B training product, so HR/L&D is the most likely first buyer.

secondary_buyer_types.reason: Line managers and company leadership can influence language training purchases when productivity or client-facing work is affected.
Sector-specific delivery may depend on external training partners or subject-matter input.

budget_owner.reason: Budget could sit in HR/L&D, a business unit, or be shared between people and operational leadership.

buyer_influencers.reason: HR/L&D usually shapes language-training procurement and reporting requirements.
Managers often decide whether a team needs sector-specific language support.
In smaller Luxembourg companies, leadership often approves training spend directly.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: INFPC co-financing of 15-35% is relevant to this product.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: Luxembourg company training co-financing exists, with 15% support and a 35% uplift for certain employees. Product-level eligibility for this exact course is not independently confirmed.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: 47% of Luxembourg employees are cross-border workers, implying roughly 228k-230k people in 2024.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: STATEC reported 489,000 employees in Luxembourg in 2024, of whom 47% were cross-border workers. The 228k figure is a rounded derivation, not the explicit official number.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Nobody does sector-specialized French plus INFPC support.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: Public substitutes exist for company-tailored and specialist language courses in Luxembourg, including Prolingua and inlingua. The absolute uniqueness claim is not defensible from the reviewed evidence.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: Industry-specific variants for finance, IT, retail, healthcare and public sector are fully ready.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: The source card describes these variants, but the public evidence reviewed does not validate that all five variants are already production-ready.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: CEFR progress reports and session recordings are sufficient proof of learning for buyers.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: Those are plausible training outputs, but no external source proves they are accepted as a formal proof standard.

funding_bodies.name: INFPC
funding_bodies.relevance: Administers Luxembourg co-financing for continuing vocational training.

funding_bodies.name: Ministry of Labour
funding_bodies.relevance: Luxembourg training-aid and linguistic leave procedures are handled through the ministry and its service portals.

regulatory_requirements.requirement: Luxembourg company training co-financing can reach 15% of annual investment, with a 35% wage-cost rate for eligible participants.

subsidies_or_funding.name: Company training co-financing
subsidies_or_funding.eligibility_notes: General employer training aid exists, but this product's exact eligibility needs provider and plan validation.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: Prolingua
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Offers tailor-made and professional-orientation courses, including specialist language for sector vocabulary.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: inlingua Luxembourg
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Offers company and individual French courses with flexible online and in-company delivery.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: Berlitz Luxembourg
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Provides corporate language solutions for international teams and multiple languages.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: INLL
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Public language institute with French offerings that can substitute for some basic workplace French needs.

legal_claim_severity.reason: The product relies on training-aid wording rather than formal accreditation or regulated-practice claims.

authority_needed_for_delivery.reason: Core delivery does not require formal approval, though sector-specific content may benefit from SME input.

authority_needed_for_claims.reason: Funding and sector-specific claims should be reviewed externally before being used in sales copy.

proof_stack.reason: B2B language products need a real buyer to prefer this format over cheaper or generic alternatives.
Progress in workplace French is the most direct intermediate proof signal.
Because substitutes exist, the product needs evidence that sector-specific French improves work outcomes.

proof_burden.reason: The product is not regulated, but it still needs commercial proof and evidence that sector specialization beats generic alternatives.

dependency_types.reason: The product is tightly tied to Luxembourg company culture and co-financing rules.
The INFPC angle is part of the positioning but needs validation per course plan.
Sector-specific French requires teachers who can handle multiple workplace domains.
Sector vocabulary for finance, healthcare or public-sector contexts benefits from subject-matter review.

scope_variance_risk.reason: The offer spans several sectors inside one country, so scope is manageable but not uniform.

expansion_variance_risk.reason: Moving this product outside Luxembourg would require reworking subsidy logic, buyer expectations and sector examples.

subsidy_exists.reason: Luxembourg company training co-financing exists and is relevant to corporate language training.

subsidy_eligibility_status.reason: The route is plausible for employer-funded language training, but the exact course setup still needs validation.

subsidy_conflict_flag.reason: No direct conflict was found between the public aid route and the product category itself.

market_regulatory_context.reason: This is a normal corporate training product, with only the funding route requiring formal checks.

claim_language_risk.reason: The sector-specialized positioning is real, but the INFPC claim needs course-specific validation.

authority_dependency_risk.reason: The product can be delivered without formal approval, but funding claims require review.

competitive_gap_risk.reason: Several corporate language substitutes already exist in Luxembourg, including specialist and tailor-made courses.

numeric_claim_risk.reason: The cross-border-worker figure is directionally correct but rounded from official employment statistics.

subsidy_conflict_risk.reason: The aid route exists, but product-level eligibility is not proven.

positioning_risk.reason: The product may look too close to generic corporate French unless sector specialization is demonstrated with real materials.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: Nobody does sector-specialized French plus INFPC support.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: Public competitors already offer specialist and tailor-made corporate language training, so the 'nobody does this' framing is false even if the exact subsidy combination remains unproven.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: All listed industry variants are ready to launch.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: The public evidence supports the general specialist-language concept, but not that every sector variant is already production-ready.

recommended_manual_review.reason: Validate the exact INFPC eligibility path before using subsidy language in sales copy.
Pick one first vertical, because the public evidence does not validate all proposed variants at once.
Replace 'nobody does this' with a more defensible value proposition tied to sector vocabulary and reporting.
